title: Senior Test Engineer
jd_text: Job SummaryQuality Assurance and Testing is an important part of the delivery of digital systems within the Health Research Authority. We have a team of skilled test professionals who make sure quality is delivered in the most effective way. We are looking to add automation engineering skills to our existing team as we value automating as much of the test effort as possible allowing our team of specialists to undertake better evaluations of the overall product set.As a Senior Test Engineer, you will create and drive the automated test engineering approach within the Research Systems Programme working closely with the Head of Test and other colleagues in the Agile development team. You will determine and implement automation approaches using Playwright(typescript) to assure, measure and build quality into every phase of the software life cycle. This involves developing or enhancing automation frameworks that help deliver scalable tests which are faster, frequent, and continuous. Job also involves performing manual testing where automation is not suitable.Main duties of the jobIdentify new and implement existing test automation frameworksWork on both functional and non-functional areas of complex system(s) built with multiple technologies (Microsoft, PEGA etc)Identify and raise awareness of defects, issues, risks arising from the automation test resultsBe proficient in a range of coding languages (C#, .Net, Java, JavaScript, Node, React, typescript etc)Work closely with members of Agile teamCollaborate with senior test analysts, business to make sure the required business scenarios are covered in the test scriptsCarry out system, integration and user acceptance testing using manual and automated techniquesLead on change impact assessment of all changes ensuring that test coverage mitigates any impact to external stakeholdersWork with the HRA's User Acceptance Team representatives and external users to perform acceptance testing of HRA software system changesManage own work within the timelines agreedDuties above are not an exhaustive list. The applicant will be expected to perform their duties as per ISTQB best practices (automation & manual)About UsAt the HRA, we are committed to fostering a supportive and inclusive working environment where we actively invest in the development and empowerment of our staff, enabling them to perform at their best. We strive to promote a healthy workplace culture where wellbeing, collaboration, and professional growth are valued and encouraged.Working at the HRA, our approach to flexible working is designed to support us all, recognising that for many people, home based or hybrid working provides us with the most productive environment to work well. We will try to offer a working pattern that suits you where possible.The HRA offers home based contracts for all locations. Newcastle based staff can be office based. For staff based in Manchester, Nottingham or Bristol, hybrid working arrangements may be possible. We encourage you to speak with us to explore how this could be accommodated.We are committed to creating an inclusive workplace that promotes and values diversity. We know from experience that different ideas, perspectives and backgrounds create a stronger, more creative workplace that helps us to deliver the best services. We welcome applications which represent the rich diversity of our community: age, disability, gender identity and expression, ethnicity, religion or belief, sex, sexual orientation and other diverse characteristics, and we have policies in place to ensure that all applicants are treated fairly and consistently at every stage of the recruitment process.DetailsDate posted17 February 2026Pay schemeAgenda for changeBandBand 7Salary£47,810 to £54,710 a yearContractFixed termDuration15 monthsWorking patternFull-time, Flexible working, Home or remote working, Compressed hoursReference number980-HRA7798917-BJob locationsBristolTemple Quay House, 2 The SquareBristolBS1 6PNJob DescriptionJob responsibilitiesJob ResponsibilitiesPlease refer to the attached job description and person specification documents for full details about this role.When completing your application, ensure you clearly demonstrate how your experience and skills align with the criteria outlined for this position.We recognise that generative AI tools can be helpful in supporting applicants during the recruitment process, particularly for tasks such as proofreading, structuring, or refining written responses. However, we encourage candidates to use AI responsibly and sparingly. Applications should reflect your authentic voice, personal experiences, and genuine qualifications. Over reliance on AI-generated content can result in generic or inaccurate submissions that fail to meet the criteria or convey your individuality. Misrepresentation of skills, knowledge, or experience, including through AI-generated content, may lead to the rejection of your application, withdrawal of your offer, or your dismissal if you do start with us. If you choose to use AI, please ensure it enhances rather than replaces your own input, and be transparent about its use where appropriate. False and insincere examples go against our values of integrity and transparency.Important information We are unable to offer employment under any sponsorship arrangement as we do not hold a sponsorship licence. Applications requiring sponsorship will not be considered.Applications that do not clearly demonstrate how the essential criteria outlined in the person specification are met will not be shortlisted.We do not accept applications via recruitment agencies or other third parties.The HRA reserves the right to close this vacancy early should it receive a sufficient number of applications.Our fixed term positions are also open to applicants who wish to apply for the role on a secondment basis. Applicants who fit this category are advised to speak with their Line Manager before applying and visit their organisations Secondments Policy.Organisational Structure And Directorate Overview The HRA is made up of five directorates: Approvals, Chief Executive, Digital, Policy and Partnerships and Resources.This role sits within HRA Digital. The digital directorate is responsible for developing, delivering and supporting complex solutions within the HRAs portfolio of UK-wide information systems for health and care research. This includes a major programme to build new systems, and to achieve the successful migration and eventual closure of current systems. The directorate owns, develops, and delivers the HRAs digital strategy in line with government digital and functional standards.The HRA Digital team is a collaborative, supportive team consisting of HRA employees, secondees, contractors and vendors.Job DescriptionOrganisational structure and directorate overview The HRA is made up of five directorates: Approvals, Chief Executive, Digital, Policy and Partnerships and Resources.This role sits within HRA Digital. The digital directorate is responsible for developing, delivering and supporting complex solutions within the HRAs portfolio of UK-wide information systems for health and care research. This includes a major programme to build new systems, and to achieve the successful migration and eventual closure of current systems. The directorate owns, develops, and delivers the HRAs digital strategy in line with government digital and functional standards.The HRA Digital team is a collaborative, supportive team consisting of HRA employees, secondees, contractors and vendors.Person SpecificationEssential Qualifications Educated to a master's level or equivalent in IS / IT field or portfolio of equivalent experience demonstrating the ability to work at this levelCertified in ISTQB FoundationDesirableCertified in ISTQB Technical Test AnalystEssential Skills Knowledge Expertise Comprehensive and detailed knowledge of test tools such as Cucumber, Playwright, K6, JMeter, Browser StackWorking knowledge of Software coding language in the Microsoft stack E.g.: C#, node.js, JavaScript, Typescript etcWorking knowledge of any other SQL and relational databasesEssential Experience Experience of working as an automated test engineer within the test team delivering successful outcomesPractical experience of testing on .NET, SQL and Microservices architecture- on prem and cloud Qualifications DesirableCertified in ISTQB Technical Test AnalystEssential Analytical and Judgemental Skills Excellent analytical and organisational skills with the ability to prioritise against a background of change and uncertainty to help derive testable scenariosEssential Knowledge, skills and Expertise Comprehensive and detailed knowledge of testing techniquesComprehensive knowledge of code version management tools like GitHub, Azure DevOpsEssential Interpersonal Skills Excellent oral and written communication skills with the ability to understand and interpret complex information. This requires Written English skills at a level to summarise complex issues into a document and Verbal English skills to be able to articulate procedural issues and comprehend complex discussion.Essential Other Commitment to inclusivity and valuing diversityEssentialAbility to work within and contribute to a high performing teamPerson SpecificationEssentialAbility to work within and contribute to a high performing teamEmployer detailsEmployer nameHealth Research AuthorityAddressBristolTemple Quay House, 2 The SquareBristolBS1 6PNEmployer's websitehttps://www.hra.nhs.uk/ (Opens in a new tab)Employer detailsEmployer nameHealth Research AuthorityAddressBristolTemple Quay House, 2 The SquareBristolBS1 6PNEmployer's websitehttps://www.hra.nhs.uk/ (Opens in a new tab)LNKD1_UKTJ

title: QA Engineer (Playwright/AI Native)
jd_text: About Foundation HealthFoundation Health is transforming healthcare through an AI-powered digital pharmacy platform that seamlessly connects operational infrastructure with high quality patient experiences. Our mission is to transform patient centric care by connecting fragmented infrastructure, optimizing care coordination, and removing friction from the patient journey. We refuse to adhere to the status quo; instead, we actively pioneer solutions that will shape the healthcare practices of tomorrow.This ambitious vision is only achievable with the dedication of the right team propelling us forward. We firmly believe that a supportive and inspiring work environment fuels creativity, transforming it into groundbreaking innovation. It is this very innovation that not only benefits our organization but also positively impacts our people, partners, and most importantly, our patients.At Foundation Health, we foster a culture that encourages our team members to broaden their horizons, urging them to bring their passion and curiosity to the workplace each day. We understand that diverse perspectives fuel progress, and we actively seek individuals who share our commitment to excellence and forward-thinking.The Mission: Quality-First HealthcareHealthcare is plagued by outdated, frustrating workflows. At Foundation Health, we believe that thoughtfully applied AI can finally simplify these systems. We are revolutionizing the pharmacy space by building deep, integrated infrastructure that changes how care is delivered.As a QA Engineer here, you aren’t just "checking boxes" - you are the guardian of a platform that directly impacts provider workdays and patient lives.The Role: Balancing Innovation with IntegrityWe are an AI-native team. We don’t just build AI; we use it to maximize our output and automate the mundane. We’re looking for a QA Engineer who leans on AI tools to supercharge their own productivity and is excited by the challenge of testing AI-powered systems.However, we also value "old school" grit. We need someone who can:Take Ownership: You’ll have the autonomy to shape our QA culture and standards from the ground up. Be the Gatekeeper: You aren't afraid to "put your foot down" if a product isn't ready for primetime. Master the Fundamentals: From deep-dive regression testing to supporting complex implementations, you ensure our move-fast culture doesn't break things. Our Tech & Your ToolkitWe’ve cleared the path with a modern, serverless stack designed for speed: Node.js, TypeScript, SvelteKit, and GCP.Automation: You’ll be able to master our Playwright frameworks. APIs: You’ll use tools like Postman to ensure our core platforms meet high-quality standards. The Future: You’ll get hands-on with Playwright MCP and Agents, with other cutting-edge tools as we ship real-world features into production. Location & Why You’d JoinWe have an office in Manchester City Centre, and while we aren't a "mandatory X days in the office" company, we believe in the power of getting together. We’re looking for Engineers who are excited to head into Manchester when needed to whiteboard, collaborate on ideas, and build rapport in person.Why leave your current role?High Agency: You’ll have a direct seat at the table to influence the technical direction of our testing practices. No Legacy Debt: You won’t be wrestling with decade-old bugs; you’ll be building new frameworks for a rapidly evolving platform. Meaningful Stake: Joining this early means taking a significant equity stake in what we are building. What You Bring3+ years of experience in QA/Software testing with a focus on web applications and APIs. Proficiency in Playwright for automated front-end testing. Experience with QA on enterprise level productsAn AI-Native Mindset: You already use AI tools to help your workflow or have experience testing AI products. Experience with Manual and Exploratory testing - you know when to automate and when to dig in by hand. Ready to build the future of healthcare quality? Let’s talk.

title: QA Engineer
jd_text: About Foundation HealthAt Foundation Health, we’re redefining what healthcare operations look like - transforming how healthcare organizations deliver pharmacy, care coordination and direct-to-patient services.Having recently raised $20m in Series A funding, we’re accelerating product development and scaling what comes next.You’d need to be comfortable working onsite 3+ days a week, in our office in Manchester City Centre.The RoleAs we scale our engineering team, this role offers a great opportunity to help shape the direction of our QA practices and develop robust testing frameworks and standards for a rapidly evolving platform. We are looking for a QA engineer who values quality, enjoys collaboration, and can bring innovative ideas to a new and dynamic team with a focus on catching issues early before they get in toWhat you will be doingWorking with our engineering team to ensure the quality and functionality of our products.Involving yourself early in the process to refine user stories and requirements prior to sprint planningCreating, executing, and maintaining test cases for our front-end applicationCollaborating on new products and core API platforms to ensure high-quality standards.Developing and maintaining automation test scripts using Cypress or Playwright.Use Postman to support testing frontend scenariosActively participating in demos and walkthroughs to offer quick feedback before mergingAssisting in shaping our QA culture, standards, and tools as part of our development process.Our tech stack includes:Node.js & TypeScript for our backend servicesSvelteKit as our front-end frameworkDocker for containerization and efficient environment managementGoogle Cloud Platform (GCP), using tools like Cloud Run and Cloud Functions for serverless operationsTerraform for Infrastructure as Code (IaC), enabling consistent, repeatable environmentsThis stack is designed for fast development cycles and scalable testing and deployment, giving us the agility to innovate and grow quickly. As a QA Engineer, you’ll have the opportunity to shape how we test and deliver quality within this environment.What we are looking forEssential3+ years of experience in QA or software testing and APIs.At least 1 year of experience in any automation testing frameworkExperience with manual and automated testing of web applications.Proficiency in either Cypress or Playwright for automated front-end testing.Experience with API testing tools like PostmanStrong ability to work independently, instinctively take ownership and communicate effectively in a remote team setting.DesirableExperience with CI/CD tools (e.g., CircleCI) to integrate tests within the development pipeline.Experience with exploratory testingImplementing cloud based mobile testing via BrowserStack or SaucelabsFamiliarity with the QA role in 3 Amigos, Root Cause Analysis and Retrospectives.Experience in the healthcare or eCommerce industries.Exposure to containerized and serverless application testing on platforms like GCP.

In your role, you will work with a part time, outside, technical consultant, specialising in the responsibilities below. This will allow for structured and greatly accelerated learning. Day to day, you will work directly with the founder, our economics lead, and our quantitative modelling lead. This is not a corporate environment. We move fast, prefer clean architecture, and value people who can think independently.Core ResponsibilitiesYou will own the technical backbone of Steadrock's internal systems:Compute Infrastructure (DGX)Manage our NVIDIA DGX server (Linux environment, CUDA, system health)Build, test, and maintain internal APIs (FastAPI/Flask)Design stable ngrok / reverse-proxy / network connections for remote accessOptimise GPU utilisation and monitor performanceAI Agent & Automation DevelopmentDevelop pipelines for fine-tuning, embeddings, inferenceBackend & Internal ToolsBuild the “Steadrock OS”: internal dashboards, scheduling tools, risk enginesMaintain Python codebase, Git repositories, environmentsConnect iPhone/iPad workflows with backend systemsData & Real Estate InterfacesBuild automated comparables enginesAssist with financial modelling scriptsWhat You NeedTechnicalStrong PythonExperience with Linux (Ubuntu), bash, SSHUnderstanding of GPUs, CUDA basics, or willingness to learn fastExperience with FastAPI, Flask or similarBasic networking knowledge (ports, tunnels, proxies)SQL or NoSQL experiencePreferredExperience with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, Qwen)Exposure to Docker or KubernetesFamiliarity with cloud providers (AWS/Azure/GCP)Coursework or experience in ML, transformers, NLP, or distributed systemsBonus (not required)Interest in real estateInterest in finance or quantitative modellingExperience building agents (LangChain, LlamaIndex, custom architectures)What We OfferWork directly with an NVIDIA DGX systemBuild AI systems from the ground upVery high autonomyExposure to private equity, high-end real estate development, planning strategyFreedom to design architecture as you see fitA fast-moving environment with no bureaucracyThe opportunity to become a foundational technical hire (progression is fastWhy This Is a Unique RoleThis is not a typical graduate software job.It is a rare opportunity to join a small, elite, multi-disciplinary team and:Build AI systems with real impactArchitect the compute backbone of a growing multi-family officeInfluence investment analysis, real estate strategy, and automationTouch every part of the tech stackGrow into a CTO-level positionYou won’t be maintaining someone else’s stack — you will be building Steadrock’s from scratch.Application ProcessCVShort Python task (we will title: Full-Stack Engineer
jd_text: Why Altium?**Altium is transforming the way electronics are designed and built. From startups to world's technology giants, our digital platforms give more power to PCB designers, supply chain, and manufacturing, letting them collaborate as never before.Constant innovation has created a transformative technology, unique in its spaceMore than 30,000 companies and 100,000 electronics engineers worldwide use AltiumWe are growing, debt-free, and financially strong, with the resources to become #1 in the EDA industry**About the Role**As a **Full-Stack Engineer** working on Octopart.com, you will be responsible for the design and maintenance of the user-facing features and interfaces, including Part Searching and Exploration. You will also develop web services implementing the business logic powering these features.Altium offers the opportunity to solve unique challenges, including complex data engineering, large-scale distributed systems, and cloud-based architectures. From concepts to building new product features, you will be able to add your own ideas to the world's most collaborative and efficient electronics design platform for electronics engineers.**Please note: The working mode is on-site: 5 days a week from our Cambridge office.****A Day in the Life** **of our Full-Stack Engineer**Building Web User Interfaces using TypeScript with modern frameworks such as Next.JS/React.Design and implement web services and manage server-side logic.Collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams, including front-end developers, data platform engineers and DevOps engineers.API scheme design with Back-end engineers is expected.Participate in code reviews and contribute to the development of coding standards and best practices.**Who We're Looking For**Proven experience as a Fullstack Software Engineer or in a similar development roleat least 4years of experience in Fullstack web application developmentHaving concrete experience in UI development with **TypeScript**Having concrete experience in Backend Development with Node.JS or other languagesUnderstanding common software design principles and patterns.Problem-solving and analytical skillsable to understand complex business processes and system workflowsAble to communicate clearly with other engineers and non-technical stakeholdersA strong sense of product ownership.**Required Technology Experience**Advanced knowledge of front-end development using **TypeScript and React** . Experience in **Next.JS** is a plus.Proficient in developing Backend Services. **Go and Python** are highly preferable.Experience in interfacing/designing web services with "Top-Down"/ "Schema-First" approach. Having experience in **GraphQL** is a significant plus.Understanding of common Relational Database (e.g. MySQL).Experience in containerized development, CI/CD, and Kubernetes is a plus.A good understanding of Elasticsearch is beneficial**Soft Skills**Excellent problem-solving and analytical abilities.Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively in a team-oriented environment.Attention to detail and strong organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.Adaptability and a commitment to continuous learning in a fast-paced environment**Our Benefits** Private health insurance including dental coverage Pension scheme with company match up to 9% nilo.health, mental health and wellbeing support Remote working abroad program Professional development support and resources Employee referral program 28 days' holiday + public holidays and special leave Home internet and remote working allowance Flexible working arrangements available based on role and location Enhanced family and special leave Corporate membership rates with national gyms Free lunch, snacks, and drinks in the office Electric car charging stations, free office parking, bicycle, and scooter storage Also, we would like you to know****We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender, gender identity or expression, or veteran status. We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace.** Learn more about why a career at Altium is an opportunity like no other:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cAYCOLpPLPE Altium Benefits** **:** https://careers.altium.com/#s-benefits Are you already an Altium employee?** Please apply directly through our internal Greenhouse job board. (https://www.greenhouse.com/) If you have questions, please contact HR.

title: Senior Full Stack Engineer
jd_text: We regularly hire for Senior Full Stack Engineer roles, so while we might not be actively recruiting right now, we’re always keen to connect with great engineers.If you’re interested, please share your CV, we’ll review it and reach out when a suitable opportunity comes up. We also make exceptions for exceptional talent, so don’t hesitate to apply. Replies may take a little longer than usual since this isn’t an active opening.About UsWe're Thrive Learning, and we’re changing the way people learn at work. We believe learning should be easy, effective, and empowering, not a chore. That’s why we built the industry’s first truly modern, all-in-one learning experience. We didn’t just create a new product; we rewrote the rulebook.Doing things differently is in our DNA. Our technology is helping over 3 million people worldwide unlock their potential and grow their careers. You've probably heard of some of the brands we work with, like Volvo, Vodafone, Krispy Kreme, and Sky. They trust us to deliver learning experiences that their people actually want to use.We’re the fastest-growing learning technology company for a reason. Do you want to be part of a team that’s building something that matters and helping people thrive?The RoleAs a Senior Full Stack Engineer, you will play a pivotal role in shaping and delivering innovative features for our enterprise-grade SaaS platforms. You’ll work across the stack, contributing to both front-end and back-end development, ensuring a seamless user experience while driving technical excellence. You’ll collaborate with Design, Product and other Engineers, and provide technical leadership to ensure high-quality, scalable solutions.Key ResponsibilitiesDeliver high-quality features and developments for SaaS products, working across front-end and back-end technologies.Provide technical leadership, supporting team members to deliver against dependable estimates while evolving best practices.Collaborate with senior stakeholders to refine and execute the technical roadmap, ensuring alignment with business priorities.Build and strengthen a developer community, contributing to guilds, sharing knowledge, and driving best practices.Actively participate in sprint planning and execution, ensuring smooth delivery of incremental improvements.Document technical approaches and maintain a focus on testing, CI/CD, and scalability.EssentialsWhat We’re Looking ForFrontend: Experience implementing web apps using React, Redux, and TypeScript.Backend: Expertise in developing microservices with Node.js and familiarity with REST, GraphQL, asynchronous messaging, and data formats like JSON.Proficiency with NoSQL databases such as MongoDB and search technologies such as Elasticsearch or Atlas Search.Strong understanding of automated testing (unit tests, integration test and test coverage) and CI/CD pipelines.Excellent communication, leadership, and mentoring skills, with experience supporting developers in a remote, collaborative environment.A pragmatic approach to problem-solving and adaptability to change.Passion for personal growth and improving ways of working.Desirable SkillsFamiliarity with AWS services (e.g SNS/SQS, S3, CloudFormation) and DevOps principles.Experience working with container technologies like Docker and orchestration platforms like AWS ECS or Kubernetes.Experience with modern frontend frameworks and tools such as Vite, Next.js.Experience with end-to-end testing tools like Cypress.Experience with backend frameworks and libraries such as Express and Apollo Server.Knowledge of Agile methodologies and delivering within a fast-paced SaaS environment.Understanding of configuring scalable and customizable features for enterprise clients.BenefitsUnlimited Holiday to support work-life balanceThrivedays—a reduced workday every Friday (10:00–15:00), with no internal meetingsFlexible Working Hours within core hours (10:00–16:00)Birthday & Christmas Off, including festive shutdown between Christmas & New YearWork From Anywhere—fully remote-friendly (4 weeks a year)Health Cash Back Plan with Health ShieldEmployee Assistance Program for confidential supportFinancial support program via MajiSalary Sacrifice Pension SchemeDeath in Service (4x annual salary)Green Car Scheme through salary sacrificeCycle to Work Scheme—save up to 42% on a new bikePerks at Work—30,000+ national & local employee discounts via CharlieHR & Health ShieldWhat To ExpectWe believe that finding a new job should be an enjoyable process. We’re passionate about bringing our whole selves to work, so what you see is what you get. All we ask from you, is that you show us your authentic self. We’re looking for people who bring something new to the team, who add to our culture and make it even greater.Initial Screening Call: If we think you're a great addition to the team, you'll be invited to an introductory call with a member of our People Team. This is a chance to tell you about Thrive and give you an opportunity to ask questions. First-Stage Interview: Following the initial call, you will have a first-stage interview with a member of the team you'd be joining. This is a chance for us to get to know you better and for you to ask any questions about the role. We'll also discuss why Thrive interests you and what you feel you can add to our team.Final Stage Interview: You will meet with the Hiring Manager and/or a senior team member for a final interview. This stage is typically used for a more in-depth discussion.At Thrive, we’re on a mission to build an inclusive, welcoming culture that encourages and actively supports diversity. Our race, gender, religion, background, sexuality and beliefs are what make us unique. Our people are expected to always treat each other with respect, care and compassion. This starts from the moment you apply for a role at Thrive Learning, so we want you know we’re an equal opportunity employer. We will always strive to be as inclusive as possible in all aspects of employment and have a zero-tolerance policy for discrimination of any kind.If you require any assistance, accommodations, or support during the application or interview stages, please don't hesitate to contact Ella Holland at ella.holland@thrivelearning.com. We're happy to help!

title: Founding AI Engineer
jd_text: OverviewAt Upsolve AI, we’re building the AI-native BI platform from first principles. We’ve already built the foundation: a modern embedded analytics platform designed to work seamlessly with LLMs and agentic systems. Now, we’re going all in on building the next generation of agentic data analysis - think “Cursor for data analytics,” integrated directly into any BI and reporting stack and designed to be embedded into our customers’ products.We’re a Y Combinator-backed (W24) startup with strong funding and support from top-tier investors (General Catalyst, Samsung Next, Soma Capital, and founders of Airtable and Handshake).The Founding TeamKa Ling Wu, Co-founder & CEO, previously led product solution development at nPlan, a Series A AI & Construction tech startup, and before that spent four years at Palantir, where she spearheaded HyperAuto and launched Palantir’s Korea operation.Serguei Balanovich, Co-founder & CTO, spent seven years at Palantir pioneering early Supply Chain and Manufacturing work and productizing it into HyperAuto.Together, we bring over 15 years of experience in data engineering and analytics, taking Palantir’s HyperAuto from inception to launch, scaling it to more than 50 enterprise customers in under 2.5 years, and having it featured in Palantir’s DPO S-1. We graduated from Y Combinator in Winter 2024.The OpportunityThis role is a key hire for Upsolve AI today. We have doubled down on building our agentic data analysis platform, and the quality of our agent performance will determine the trajectory of the business.As our founding AI engineer, you’ll work directly with the CTO to design and ship real-world LLM/agent solutions. You’ll run experiments, evaluate outcomes, and set product direction based on what proves successful. You won’t just write code you’ll be the deciding voice for what we build, how we build it, and why.This is not a research role. It’s a high-impact product engineering role in forward-deployed style where your work ships into production and is used by customers daily.Your first 12 months at Upsolve AI will involve...Designing, prototyping, and deploying agentic LLM systems into production with real customer workloads.Running experiments to explore architectures, frameworks, and approaches - and driving product direction from their results.Building evaluation, observability, and improvement pipelines for agent quality and reliability.Integrating with our modern BI platform and customer-facing workflows.Establishing best practices for LLM/agent development, from tooling to performance monitoring.Embedding and collaborating directly with users and customers to validate what “good” looks like in production.What You Bring To Upsolve AI7+ years of engineering experience, including deep experience shipping production LLM systems.2+ years track record of building agentic or multi-step reasoning systems that work in complex, real-world environments.Strong command of modern AI/LLM tooling (LangChain, Mastra, DSPy, vector DBs, eval frameworks, observability, OpenAI/Anthropic APIs, fine-tuning).Strong engineering ability (TypeScript preferred).Experience with databases, data warehouses, data systems, data connections, and big data engineering/analytics.Product mindset: ability to make opinionated calls, weigh trade-offs, and push for what delivers real customer value.Comfort working in an early-stage environment: high ownership, ambiguity, and rapid iteration.What we value and what’s in it for you?At Upsolve AI, we operate like a high-performance sports team. Hard work is required for success, but when we move together, we don’t just win games, we win championships. We despise bureaucracy and redundant process. Everyone here is a builder who rolls up their sleeves. We value ownership and trust: the person closest to the problem has the authority to solve it. We believe each team member brings a unique strength, and we shape our work to amplify those spikes.In This Role, You Will GetImpact: This is a defining role. You’ll be shaping the core AI product that determines our product trajectory.Upside: Competitive salary plus founding-level equity.Culture: Low bureaucracy, high trust, and high ownership.Hybrid flexibility: Our office is in Shoreditch, and we prefer to work together face to face when possible because we believe that’s how magic happens. That said, we highly value flexibility and working from home one or two days a week is no problem.AdditionalSalary range of £100k–140k / 1–3% equity (depending on experience and package).Opportunity to work with an elite team of entrepreneurs and engineers.Take-what-you-need holiday policy: startup life is intense, and we want you to have the agency to rest and recharge.Location: Central London office with flexible hybrid work.

title: Backend Engineer (ai Platform) - London
jd_text: Backend Engineer (AI Platform) - London£80k–£100k + EMI equityAbout the RoleWe’re hiring a Backend Engineer to build the core infrastructure behind Revax’s AI-native platform for professional services.You’ll design, optimise, and scale the systems that power multi-agent drafting, knowledge graphs, and auditable AI workflows used by accountants, tax advisors, and consultants at leading firms. This is not a “ticket-taking” role -you’ll work directly with the founders, own critical backend services end-to-end, and help define how AI systems are built responsibly in regulated domains.If you enjoy designing clean APIs, scaling Python services, and building systems that actually ship to paying customers, this role is for you.Compensation: £80,000–£100,000 base + EMI equityLocation: London About RevaxRevax is building a new class of AI-native software for tax advisory firms.Today, professional services teams spend countless hours drafting, reviewing, and re-drafting work - constrained by fragmented knowledge, manual workflows, and brittle tooling. We turn a firm’s own expertise into structured, auditable, AI-powered systems that draft, analyse, and explain deliverables with full provenance.We’re a fast-moving, well-funded startup backed by leading angels, already working with major firms, and building at the intersection of AI agents, knowledge graphs, and real-world enterprise workflows.What You’ll DoBuild & own core backend systems·      Build and optimise databases for complex, real-world data·      Design and implement APIs using FastAPI for AI workflows and product features·      Build and scale LangGraph-based multi-agent systems with checkpointing, retries, and observability·      Integrate and scale vector search, knowledge graphs (Neo4j), and associated retrieval pipelines into production services·      Deploy and operate GPU-backed services for AI workloads (e.g. inference, embeddings, or agent pipelines) in productionScale & operate production infrastructure·      Deploy services on GCP (Cloud Run / GKE) or equivalent cloud infrastructure·      Improve performance, reliability, and cost efficiency (latency, throughput, retries, backoffs)·      Implement security-first patterns: auth flows, service-to-service auth, IAM, and access controls·      Help prepare the platform for enterprise customers (SSO, auditability, compliance ready patterns)Collaborate closely with founders & product·      Shape system architecture and API design alongside full-stack and ML engineers·      Translate ambiguous product ideas into robust backend implementations·      Iterate quickly based on real customer usage and feedbackWhat We’re Looking ForEssential·      2-3 years building backend systems for real users·      Production experience with FastAPI (or similar modern Python frameworks)·      Solid understanding of API design, PostgreSQL, and distributed systems fundamentals·      Experience deploying and operating services on cloud infrastructure (GCP, AWS, and Azure)·      High ownership mindset: you care deeply about reliability, correctness, and outcomesStrong Plus·      Experience with LangGraph, RAG pipelines, vector databases, or knowledge graphs·      Familiarity with Neo4j or graph-based data modelling·      Experience working on AI-native or agentic systems·      Comfort working in early-stage startups with ambiguity and speed·      Experience deploying and working with GPU services for AI workloadsBonus·      Exposure to professional services, finance, tax, or regulated workflows·      You actively use modern AI coding tools (Cursor, Claude Code, etc.)·      Strong instincts for developer experience and internal toolingWhy Revax·      Real ownership over core backend infrastructure·      Work directly with founders on high-impact technical decisions·      AI-native product with real customers and immediate feedback·      Modern stack: Python, FastAPI, LangGraph, Postgres, Supabase, Neo4j, GCP·      Meaningful early-stage equity: we hire owners, not passengers·      A lean, low-ego, high drive team that ships fast and cares about their workPractical details·      London based (strong preference for in office collaboration)·      Visa sponsorship available

title: Software Engineer – Full Stack
jd_text: Work with purpose.WorkBoard’s Strategy Execution Platform powers the digital operating rhythm for companies around the globe, providing organization-wide clarity, alignment, and insights for growth. AstraZeneca, Ford, 3M, Intel and many others rely on WorkBoard’s platform, playbook, and expertise to accelerate results by aligning OKRs, simplifying business reviews and scorecards, focusing weeklies on outcomes, and leveraging analytics – all with embedded AI. More than 15,000 people are certified in WorkBoard’s OKR coaching and Outcome Mindset Methodology™ which enables their organizations to quickly gain the agility OKRs can provide.Based in Silicon Valley and founded in 2013, WorkBoard investors include Andreessen Horowitz, SoftBank, GGV Capital, Workday Ventures, M12 (Microsoft), Intel Capital, Silicon Valley Bank, and Capital One.THE OPPORTUNITYAs a Full Stack Software Engineer, you will help build and enhance WorkBoard’s platform by contributing across frontend and backend systems. You’ll work closely with engineering manager, product managers, and designers to deliver features that are scalable, reliable, and impactful for customers.Experience building AI-powered features or using AI tools to improve development workflows is a plus.What You’ll Do Contribute high-quality code across backend and frontend systems Build and maintain scalable APIs, services, and data models Collaborate with product and design partners to deliver user-facing features Participate in code reviews and design discussions with the engineering team Help investigate, debug, and resolve performance or reliability issues Translate product requirements into clear technical solutions and well-scoped Jira tickets Follow and contribute to best practices around code quality, testing, and observability Explore new tools and approaches—including AI-enabled workflows—where they add valueWhat You Bring 3–5 years of professional software engineering experience with full stack exposure Strong experience with Node.js and modern JavaScript/TypeScript development Experience building user interfaces with React and modern frontend patterns Experience using Claude Code OR Cursor for software development and code generation Production experience with PHP and MySQL is plus Working knowledge of Python for backend services, scripting, or integrations Familiarity with deploying and operating systems on Azure Cloud Solid problem-solving and debugging skills across the stack Strong collaboration and communication skillsNice to Have Solid design principles and coding fundamentals Experience using AI tools to improve engineering velocity and code quality Exposure to DevOps and CI/CD practices Familiarity with LangGraph, LangChain, or similar agent-based frameworksWithin Your First 6 Months1 Month – Ramp up on systems, ship initial contributions, and build context across the platform and team.3 Months – Own features end-to-end with support from teammates, contribute actively to design discussions, and deepen full stack expertise.6 Months – Drive meaningful projects independently, improve engineering practices, and help raise the bar on performance, scalability, and thoughtful AI adoption.We are a no-ego bunch, and super excited to build an awesome team in a category-creating company together!OUR VALUES – WE LIVE BY THE 4 Hs Humble experts Hungry for the opportunity Intellectually honest Operating as one happy teama Few Of Our Awesome Benefits Discretionary time off & sick days Paid holidays Health insurance 401(k) with employer matching Quarterly All-Hands meetings And much more!We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace committed to building a team culture that celebrates learning, diversity and inclusion. If you’re hungry to grow your skills while growing a company, your sense of urgency matches the size of our market opportunity, and you value and enable team mates’ contributions, then come join us!The US base salary range for this full-time position is $100k annual salary .... Our salary ranges are determined by role, level and geographic location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Please note that the compensation details listed in US role postings reflect the base salary only, and do not include equity, or benefits.
